story: Henry returns to his childhood house, its rooms hushed since his father went into hospital on January 15. The hall is unchanged: an old telephone on directories, framed sea prints, a plastic calendar frozen on that date. He climbs the creaking stairs. High above, a small brown bird, trapped since morning, flits along the joists in frantic starts and stops. He turns into his old bedroom, steps into the bathroom, and steams the mirror. A sudden noise sounds behind him.
implausible_knowledge: High above, a small brown bird, trapped since morning, flits along the joists in frantic starts and stops.
question: When Henry hears the sudden noise upstairs, what will he do?
answer_without_knowledge: Rush to look for an intruder.
